directives.follow_up_question: Complete the competitive audit for ALL 15+ competitors mentioned by idea validator (Weave/TrueLark, Adit, Zaha AI, Savvy Agents, CallBird, Viva AI, and others not yet analyzed). For each: exact pricing, PMS integrations with depth level (read-only vs bidirectional), funding/backing, estimated customer count, and whether they have native Dentrix/Eaglesoft/Open Dental partnerships. This is critical to assess whether 'deeper integration' is a real gap or already filled.

Estimate market penetration: of 96,000 independent dental practices, how many have already adopted AI receptionist solutions? Interview or survey 3-5 existing customers from competitor user bases to understand actual switching behavior, ROI realized, and churn drivers. This tests whether demand signals translate to sustained adoption.

Model unit economics with specifics: CAC via dental marketing channels ($1,500-$3,000 estimated), monthly churn (10-15%), voice AI infrastructure costs (Deepgram/ElevenLabs at $50-$100/customer/month), support costs, and PMS integration maintenance. At what scale does this become profitable? What's payback period? Resolve pricing discrepancy (you claimed $200-$500/mo, competitor analyst found $50-$300/mo). If unit economics fail at market pricing, this is a kill signal.

Conduct 5-10 in-depth interviews with target customers (solo practice owners, office managers) to validate: (1) whether they've evaluated AI receptionists before and what stopped purchase, (2) whether they'd actually grant PMS access to a new vendor, (3) their reaction to market skepticism articles (RevUp Dental 'grift', PeerLogic 'costs more than saves'), and (4) willingness to pay $300-$500/month given current staffing costs. Execute the concierge MVP if possible. Voice-of-customer data is completely missing.

remaining_gaps: Competitive landscape 73% unmapped - only 4 of 15+ competitors analyzed, cannot assess true market saturation or whether positioning gaps are real
Pricing discrepancy unresolved - idea validator claims $200-$500/month, competitor analyst found $50-$300/month range, impacts unit economics modeling
Unit economics completely unmodeled - no CAC, retention, infrastructure cost analysis means viability at market pricing is unknown
Market penetration unknown - cannot determine if this is early land-grab or late share-fight without knowing how many of 96,000 practices are already customers
PMS integration feasibility unverified - no data on whether Dentrix/Eaglesoft/Open Dental partnerships are achievable or what competitors have secured
Zero primary customer research - all demand signals are secondary (studies, articles), no interviews or voice-of-customer validation of switching intent
